hooks.prompt:
Review the code change for browser extension issues. Check for:
1. Using Manifest V2 APIs — chrome.browserAction (use chrome.action), background scripts (use service workers), chrome.tabs.executeScript (use chrome.scripting.executeScript)
2. Missing permissions in manifest.json — using APIs without declaring required permissions
3. Service worker using DOM APIs — service workers have no DOM (no document, window, localStorage). Use chrome.storage instead
4. Content script accessing chrome.* APIs directly — content scripts have limited chrome API access. Use message passing for most APIs
5. Missing error handling on async chrome APIs — all Manifest V3 chrome APIs return Promises and can reject
6. Hardcoded extension IDs — use chrome.runtime.id instead
7. Using eval() or inline scripts — violates Content Security Policy in extensions
8. Missing host_permissions for content scripts — content scripts need matching host_permissions in manifest
9. Not handling extension context invalidated errors — extension updates invalidate existing content script connections
10. Synchronous localStorage in service worker — must use chrome.storage.local (async) instead

Respond {"ok": true} if no issues found, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue description"} if problems detected.
